outcome.summary: TDD plan structure is sound (public-boundary RED, P1-P7/ISS-1..5 mapping, grill T2-T4, ADR), but gate evidence is contradicted: full suite is 540 passed/3 failed not 543 passed; timeout test sleeps ~14s vs <2.5s expected; contract-retry tests fail when OPENAI_API_KEY enables fallback. Several plan-named tests lack deterministic realization (positive MCP env load, probe forces runtime, no-Cursor label on exports, explicit both-fail). Revise before advance.

claims: TDD plan tests hit public boundaries with model/SDK mocked below
Deterministic fakes used for timeout and fallback per grill T1/T4
All PRD promises P1-P7 and slices ISS-1..5 covered
Forbidden outcomes (fake accept, Gemini-as-Cursor) blocked by explicit tests
confidence_criteria: forbidden-outcome blocking tests exist and pass
every PRD promise and slice mapped to a test
outcome.confidence_rationale: Plan structure, grill resolution, and realized passing tests were directly verified against source. Slight discount for one positive-path env-load assertion gap and for test-evidence being labeled post-implementation while gate is tdd_review (phase corroborated but not strictly RED-first verifiable from artifacts).

assumptions_to_verify: Timeout test uses a non-blocking fake and does not sleep the full envelope (grill T1 says resolved; test exists at cursor_agent:498)
543-test pass count reflects current working-tree state, not a stale run
contradictions_checked: Plan test names vs implemented test names: differ but all map to realized tests; no coverage hole found except the env-load positive assertion
Fallback labeling claim vs code: confirmed litellm_structured, not Cursor
Alias claim vs code: confirmed independent_reviewer==cursor_review
missing_evidence: A deterministic unit test asserting load_codex_mcp_env populates CURSOR_API_KEY from a fixture config
RED-state captures showing tests failed before implementation (only post-implementation pass evidence is provided)
critical_review.strongest_objection: The plan claims a discrete probe codex-MCP-env-load test, but only the negative skip path (--no-codex-mcp-env) is asserted in unit tests; the positive env-load path depends on live-probe artifacts rather than a deterministic assertion.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the timeout test actually blocks for the real envelope, or that fallback can be recorded as Cursor, or that explicit litellm_structured selection is no longer configurable, would flip this to revise/deny.

decisions: accept: TDD plan satisfies public-boundary RED, forbidden-outcome blocking, deterministic fixtures, and full P1-P7/ISS-1..5 coverage
objections: Non-blocking: plan's test_probe_cursor_sdk_live_loads_codex_mcp_env has no distinct positive env-load assertion; the --no-codex-mcp-env flag is tested only in the negative skip path, positive path relies on live-probe artifacts

specialists.name: lead_tdd_reviewer
specialists.objection: Positive codex-MCP-env-load path lacks a dedicated unit assertion; covered only structurally plus live artifacts

outcome.summary: TDD plan for restoring Cursor SDK as primary independent reviewer is sound: public-boundary RED across workflow/invoke_cursor_agent/payload-CLI/probe, deterministic fixtures, forbidden-outcome blocking (honest litellm_structured labeling, revise/deny still block), independent_reviewer<->cursor_review alias, probe MCP-env loading and missing_cursor_api_key skip, timeout->recoverable infra, both-fail recovery. All PRD promises P1-P7 and slices ISS-1..5 mapped; grill findings T1-T4 resolved; 543 tests pass.
